pr.title: docs: fix stale CLI examples in README enterprise integration section
pr.body:
## Summary Fix two broken CLI examples in the README's Enterprise Integration section that reference flags which do not exist in the actual CLI. ## Why During repository onboarding and health check, I found two CLI examples in the Enterprise Integration `
` section that use non-existent flags: 1. `--reporter json --log-level info` — these flags don't exist; the correct flag is `--json` 2. `-o output.txt` — this flag doesn't exist; the binary uses shell redirection for file output Additionally, the Task Scheduler example referenced `C:\Program Files\Windows-MTR\mtr.exe` (an MSI install path), but the current docs recommend portable ZIP install to a user-chosen directory. ## Changes - [x] Documentation updated Two line changes in `README.md`: - **Task Scheduler example**: Replaced non-existent `-o output.txt` flag and updated path from `C:\Program Files\Windows-MTR\` to `C:\Tools\windows-mtr\` (matching portable install docs) - **Centralized Monitoring example**: Replaced non-existent `--reporter json --log-level info` with valid `--json -c 10` ## Testing ```bash cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings # PASS (exit 0) cargo check --workspace # PASS (exit 0) ``` No code changes — documentation-only fix. Verified both replacement commands match actual CLI flags defined in `src/main.rs` (`-j/--json`, `-c`, `-r`). ## Compatibility / Risk Notes - CLI compatibility impact: None (documentation only) - Output/report format impact: None - Operational risk and rollback plan: Zero risk, single-file doc change ## Security Considerations None — documentation-only change. ## Checklist - [x] I followed the contribution guidelines in `CONTRIBUTING.md` - [x] I ran relevant checks locally - [x] I updated docs/changelog as needed - [x] I confirmed no sensitive data is included Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/871fb66468ba497e98d69036cb2a90fc Requested by: @benjisho ---
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
